title: SPLIT Gendo Shinji and Rei in NME
display_title: SPLIT Gendo Shinji and Rei in NME
wikitext:
[]: SPLIT: Gendo, Shinji & Rei in NME             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Tue Jan 10, 2012 7:25 am SPLIT From this thread . Azathoth wrote: But I mean it does make sense, even if Anno didn't immediately see why. Gendou is responsible for creating Shinji's mother-lust behavioral loop (scary father, better run to mommy) so here he perpetuates by making it possible for Shinji never to have to leave mommy. Gendou of NGE was not really responsible in some sense for Shinji being a failure - I mean, he was, but no malice aforethought - indeed, he thought he was doing the poor kid a favor (and might have been as far as we know) - at any rate, Gendou of NGE does not care about Shinji, he uses Shinji because he has no other choice. Gendou of NME is a more "sinister" figure in that he is deliberately manipulating Shinji in order to stave off Shinji's adulthood, he is trying to force him to remain a child at the same time as he demands that Shinji grow up. In some ways he is a more realistic father, reflecting that sort of paradox, trying to balance his son and his wife-substitute and coming off as irresponsible to both; whereas Gendou of NGE simply did not care about either, only the image of his wife inside his head. In Ha we discover that NME Gendou's plan makes sense on a macro (Instrumentality-related) level as well as on the level of being Shinji's father. In order to obtain that security of perpetual infancy, Shinji will gladly end the world - for the sake of a world-eating mother, Shinji will do anything, because this universe does not (yet) contain a competent Asuka-substitute to resolve his problem. Includes awakening Unit 01 and all that. Gendou uses Rei as carrot and himself as stick, driving Shinji wherever he can be made to go. That's the point of the line in Jo, regardless of whether Anno had the details yet.

[]:             Quote Post by chee » Tue Jan 10, 2012 10:09 am Jornophelanthas wrote: The metaphor of Third Impact as human conception rather than death implies a significant change in tone from this. Especially if the child that is conceived is the human race itself. Remember that the human race in Rebuild is dying, as no new children are being born. Hope is dying fast .

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Tue Jan 10, 2012 10:24 am chee wrote: Wait what

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Tue Jan 10, 2012 3:58 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: And is Jorno talking about how birth rates declined post-2I? I guess that's still an important part factoid in NME like it was in NGE, but I don't recall it being mentioned in 1.11. Was it in 2.22?

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Tue Jan 10, 2012 4:22 pm SaltyJoe wrote: But i think this tangent isn't really addressing Jorno's main points.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Tue Jan 10, 2012 5:04 pm I do recall Shinji and Rei's sibling-like relationship--along with Gendo's plans for them--being discussed several times before. Especially in the case of Near Third Impact with "sins of the father being carried over to the son" (Shinji is ironically taking Gendo's advice without realizing it--and look where it gets him). As well as the various Oedipal implications of the Shinji/Rei/Gendo/Eva-01-Yui relationships. Shinji and Rei and any "quickening" has also been mentioned in the possible context of Eva-08 being a revamped Eva-01 (Eva-08 does suspiciously appear to have duel hatch entry plugs)... Unfortunately, all this was mentioned all over the place in different threads over the past 3 years so it's hard to pinpoint where they are, but fresh discussion never hurt. :) I'm partial to the idea that Gendo's 3I plans involve a "happy family" scenario where Shinji/Rei (assuming she's Lilith) /Yui and Gendo stay within Eva-01 for all eternity, somewhat of a "step above" from Gendo's NGE plans of him and Yui within Eva-01 forever. What this means for the rest of humanity's fate and if 3I/Instrumentality mechanics work differently from NGE, wait and see. Granted, either scenario is downbeat for various reasons, and it would be twisted love for Gendo to FORCE Shinji and Rei into such a situation against their own free will.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jan 10, 2012 7:23 pm Na7e wrote: Japan also historically reacted to natural disasters with a negative population growth.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Tue Jan 10, 2012 9:44 pm SSD it always seemed like Anno was reaffirming a non-sexual sibling relationship in NME. None of the acts Shinji did were overly romantic. The finally with Shinji and Rei only presses this home as they retreat into the mothers womb. I definitely see the big happy family scenario as a possibility though. Rei's origins aren't normal in the 'when a mommy and, daddy love each other very much'. I believe it was in 2.0 they do a cut in which it switchs from Rei to Yui and, they look exactly alike, and I mean mirror image. Then there's the next time preview. Which kind of shows that somethings up. So I think that the re-birth when they escape the Eva. Would be the re-evaluation of the relationship as brother/sister.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Tue Jan 10, 2012 10:10 pm Na7e wrote: Why yes I do. I didn't know if I should link it. It was a paper by someone who from Harvard.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 11, 2012 12:19 am Na7e wrote: SSD it always seemed like Anno was reaffirming a non-sexual sibling relationship in NME. None of the acts Shinji did were overly romantic. The finally with Shinji and Rei only presses this home as they retreat into the mothers womb. I definitely see the big happy family scenario as a possibility though.

[]:             Quote Post by Na7e » Wed Jan 11, 2012 1:23 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Mh. I'd much rather we get a "happy end" in Final of Gendo/Shinji/Rei living on (maybe Shinji dating Shikinami Asuka too, depending on how their development goes!) despite Yui staying within Eva (whether she flies into space again or who knows what) instead of whatever Gendo's plan is.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Jan 11, 2012 1:54 am @Na7e, As mentioned in PM, thanks for sending me the link of the Harvard paper written in 2004. @everyone, The paper studied two countries, Japan and Italy, with data gathered from late 18th century to mid-20th century. One of the conclusions is that in both countries natural disasters led to decrease in fertility rate.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 11, 2012 9:01 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: Yeah. :) In 1.0 as well, there are hints of Rei/Shinji having something of a sibling relationship: the first Hell Train has Gendo and Yui's naming their child talk from NGE Episode 20 before we hear Rei's voice repeating the names all mixed up ("Shinji...Shinji...Ayanami. Shinji. Rei...Rei...Ikari. Rei." etc) and we see the bug-eyed Rei.

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Jan 11, 2012 9:07 am ^ What you said can also be applied to a sibling "reunion" or "complementation" though. I am also for SSD's idea that the theme of potential sibling between Shinji and Rei that was mostly only hinted at in NGE could be explored further and made more prominent in NME, but your idea can also be made to work I guess, so it is just a matter of personal preference.

[]:             Quote Post by Jornophelanthas » Wed Jan 11, 2012 9:18 am symbv wrote: ^ What you said can also be applied to a sibling "reunion" or "complementation" though. I am also for SSD's idea that the theme of potential sibling between Shinji and Rei that was mostly only hinted at in NGE could be explored further and made more prominent in NME.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Wed Jan 11, 2012 12:55 pm Hehe, while I prefer Shinji and Rei staying as themselves, a Shinji-Rei union would also certainly twist around various Instrumentality-triggered terms (which first appeared in Episode 20, when Shinji was dissolved in Eva-01 after Zeruel) like so (Emoticons used for slight levity) : Ikari-kun, don't you wish to become one with me? Wh-what?! Don't you wish to become one in body and soul? It can be very, very pleasant. A-alright. Ayanami. *After soul-merging* Being one with Ikari-kun in body and soul makes me feel warm and fuzzy. Th-this isn't what I thought you meant, Ayanami...

[]:             Quote Post by symbv » Wed Jan 11, 2012 9:09 pm Good 4-koma material... Well, since "union" between Shinji and Rei did happen in EoE, perhaps a version of it will also happen in NME -- but how you interpret it (mother? sister? lover?) may be subject to debate...
